Much heated discussion was indulg-
ed in by members of the trades coun-
ell at the meeting in Central Labor
hall last night, the action Of the mu-
sicians In attempting to boycott the
o. X. concert hall and of the bar
tenders in allowing their men to re-
turn to work when the place was on
the unfair list being the principal
topics. The musicians put through a
motion deprecating the action of the
bartenders. but failed on a motion
to call the bartenders out again.


The report of the carnival commit
tee was referred to an auditing COM
mittee.


a report was read from the tele-
phone girls regarding their share OF
the receipts from the sales of the
country store when was conducted
by them and the retail clerks Jointly
Out OF the total receipts OF s42S. &C
they protested that they only receiv.
ed as their share $500.


c. w. Dickinson of the Fedora
union. Miss Murphy of the retail
clerks and Mr.. Bareutner OF the mu
sicians presented credentials AM
were given seats.


The names of four unfair members
! OF the street car union were pre-
scented and the new scale of the black,
smiths union was endorsed.
